output.summary: Make preset filter pills always render as non-interactive spans regardless of displayFilters setting. When displayFilters=true, the active filters bar should only show user-selected (non-preset) filters as interactive pills. The .archive-preset-filters container should always render when presets are active.

fileChanges.description: Always render .archive-preset-filters when presets are active; exclude preset terms from .archive-active-filters
fileChanges.details: 1. Move the .archive-preset-filters block (currently lines 344-360) so it renders whenever presets are active, not just when !$displayFilters. Place it immediately after the .archive-filters div (before the loading spinner), conditioned only on `!empty($preset_filters) && $has_selected_option`. 2. In the .archive-active-filters loop (lines 325-338), add a check to skip terms that exist in $preset_filters for the current taxonomy. Only render interactive <button> pills for terms NOT in the preset list. This means: inside the foreach over $selected_options[$taxonomy_slug], add `if (!empty($preset_filters[$taxonomy_slug]) && in_array($term, $preset_filters[$taxonomy_slug], true)) { continue; }` before rendering the button. 3. The .archive-active-filters container's :has(button:only-child) CSS rule will auto-hide it if only the Clear All button remains (i.e., all active filters are presets).

fileChanges.description: Exclude preset filter values from interactive pill generation in buildButtons()
fileChanges.details: In the buildButtons() function (around line 280-313), when iterating over the values array for each select, skip values that are present in presetFilters[field]. Specifically, after `var values = input.value.split(',').filter(...)` add filtering: `var userValues = values.filter(function(v) { return !(presetFilters[field] && presetFilters[field].indexOf(v) !== -1); });` Then use userValues instead of values for creating the interactive button pills. The preset values should still remain in the hidden input (they still apply as query filters), but should NOT generate interactive pills in .archive-active-filters. Also in the Clear All handler, after clearing hidden inputs, the preset filter values should be restored to the hidden inputs (since presets should persist). Add logic: after resetting all hidden inputs to '', re-populate them with preset values: `Object.keys(presetFilters).forEach(function(key) { var input = form.querySelector('input[data-type="' + key + '"]'); if (input && presetFilters[key].length) { input.value = presetFilters[key].join(','); } });`

fileChanges.description: Update comment on preset filter pill styles to reflect new behavior
fileChanges.details: Change the comment on line 230 from '/* ── Preset filter pills (displayFilters=false, non-interactive) ── */' to '/* ── Preset filter pills (always non-interactive) ── */'. No functional CSS changes needed -- existing styles for .archive-preset-pill already handle non-interactive appearance correctly.

designDecisions.decision: Preset filter pills are always non-interactive spans, never interactive buttons, regardless of displayFilters setting
designDecisions.evidence: User explicitly stated preset pills should ALWAYS be non-interactive

designDecisions.decision: The .archive-preset-filters container renders whenever presets are active, not conditionally on displayFilters
designDecisions.evidence: User stated the preset container should always render when presets are active

designDecisions.decision: Clear All only clears user-selected filters, not preset filters. Preset values are restored to hidden inputs after clear.
designDecisions.evidence: Presets are non-interactive and should persist; only user-selected filters should be clearable

designDecisions.decision: The .archive-active-filters bar auto-hides via CSS :has(button:only-child) when all active filters are presets (only Clear All button remains)
designDecisions.evidence: Archive.css line 32-34 already has this rule; it naturally handles the case where no user-selected filters exist
